key_insight: A physics-informed multihead neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold for PDE solution families, revealing robust principal directions and frequency patterns beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a compact, reproducible way to summarize and compare parameterized PDE dynamics, which can support reduced-order modeling and faster analysis.
method: Shared PINN body learns a latent embedding of the solution space.
Linear heads reconstruct individual PDE solutions for different initial conditions.
A head-orthogonalization penalty removes latent degeneracies and stabilizes the basis across runs.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, a latent size of 20 compressed to about 2–4 principal components for 95% variance and 4–7 for 99%, with similar qualitative behavior for heat and wave equations.
watch_item: The latent coordinates are not unique because orthogonalization still leaves a basis-change freedom, so interpretation should rely on the invariant spectral profiles rather than individual components alone.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
